name: Ra-Pay MCP Server — Fiat Payments via Stripe
what_it_does: MCP server enabling AI agents to send fiat payments via Stripe Connect with a two-step confirmation flow. Designed for agent-initiated payments with human-in-the-loop confirmation — agents can initiate payment flows, pause for confirmation, then execute via Stripe.
use_cases: Agent-initiated payment workflows with human confirmation gates
Automating vendor or freelancer payments from business process agents
Building AI-powered expense and reimbursement workflows with Stripe backend
Testing and prototyping agent payment capabilities in development environments
not_for: Fully autonomous payments without human confirmation (requires two-step confirm)
Consumer-facing payment collection (Stripe fee and terms apply)
best_when: A business wants AI agents to initiate payments as part of automated workflows — while keeping humans in the confirmation loop before money actually moves.
avoid_when: Autonomous agent payments without human review. FINANCIAL RISK: This server moves real money via Stripe — always test with Stripe test mode before production.

auth.notes: Stripe Connect credentials required. Uses Stripe test/live mode. Two-step confirmation flow is built into the MCP server design — do not bypass.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free open source; Stripe fees apply per transaction (2.9% + 30¢)
pricing.paid_tiers: None for MCP server; Stripe charges apply
pricing.notes: MCP server is free open source. Real payment costs are Stripe transaction fees. Use test mode for development.

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Sends real money — ALWAYS use Stripe test mode during development
Two-step confirmation is intentional — do not modify to bypass human review
PCI compliance: Stripe handles card data but Ra-Pay is in the payment flow chain
Stripe Connect requires platform account setup before use
Community implementation — verify against current Stripe Connect API
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Real fiat payment execution. Two-step confirmation is a safety feature — do not bypass. PCI compliance required. Stripe credentials must be secured.

security_score_components.security_notes: Payment execution with real money. PCI scope. Stripe credentials critical. Two-step flow required for safety. HTTPS enforced.
